key_insight: The paper shows that a transformer-based flow matching model in VAE latent space can generate high-resolution, path-dependent plastic stress fields in a single step.
engineering_relevance: This offers a much faster surrogate than finite element analysis for geometry- and loading-history-dependent solid mechanics simulations, with substantial CPU and GPU speedups.
method: Models plastic field evolution as video synthesis over all time steps.
Uses flow matching with a non-Gaussian source distribution to reduce path crossings and enable one-step sampling.
Adds token-level loading embeddings and two auxiliary networks to improve path-dependent prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate generation even with limited training data and runtime gains of about 6 to 7 times over FEM on CPUs and roughly two orders of magnitude on consumer GPUs.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the chosen VAE latent representation and training coverage of loading paths, so unseen path histories or geometries could still be challenging.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
